hsk2_126.titleEn: Phrase interrogative - 用“还是”连接的选择问句
hsk2_126.objectiveEn: Les **phrases interrogatives** couvrent les questions en 吗, les questions A不A, les mots interrogatifs (什么, 哪儿, 怎么, 为什么…).
usageEn: Pour **用**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **用什么？** (De quoi s'agit-il ?) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Pour **还是**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **...还是...？** (... ou ... ?) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Après substitution, vérifier que 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 restent cohérents avec le sens et le temps de la phrase.
Contexte : surtout en questions et réponses et questions, réactions et modalité.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk2_135.titleEn: Sentence Components (Type of Sentence) - 强调时间、地点、方式、动作者
hsk2_135.objectiveEn: The **Is...** structure serves to **highlight** an element of an event (moment, place, manner, agent, etc.) that is often already completed. It does not express the sense of identity 'be', but rather a **highlight**.
usageEn: For **location**, take a model sentence like **What place?** (What place?) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**What place?**) and replace the subject/objects/times while keeping the same structure.
Context: mainly in questions and responses.
notesEn: place — Adverb: Turns a noun phrase into an adverb (study seriously).
**location** — place; location.
**way** — method; way.

hsk2_136.titleEn: Pivot Construction - Command Sentences: Subject + Please/Call/Allow... + Object 1 + Verb + Object 2
hsk2_136.objectiveEn: The **pivot sentences** (Pivot sentences) have an element that is both the object of the 1st verb and the subject of the 2nd (e.g., Let him go / Please help me).
usageEn: Start with the template: **Subject + Please**.
Placement: strictly follow the order of blocks indicated by the template, from left to right.
Context: mainly in oral interactions and instructions.
examplesEn: Let the children play a bit.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk2_137.titleEn: Double Object Sentence - Subject + Verb + Give + Object 1 + Object 2
hsk2_137.objectiveEn: The **double-object sentences** (Double-object sentences) use verbs like Give / Send / Tell to express **give / tell**: V + someone + something.
usageEn: Start with the template: **Subject + Verb + Give + Object 1 + Object 2**.
Placement: strictly follow the order of blocks indicated by the template, without moving the blocks.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk2_138.objectiveEn: Here we deal with **complex phrases** (complex sentences): how to connect two propositions (cause, condition, contrast, purpose, etc.).
usageEn: For **is**, take a model sentence like **... or ...?** (... or ...?) and replace only the variable information.
For **or**, take a model sentence like **... or ...?** (... or ...?) and replace only the variable information.
Context: mainly in questions and responses.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk2_139.objectiveEn: The **contrast/concession phrases** (Contrast/concession phrases) connect two opposite ideas: Although... but... / Despite... still... / Although... yet... / However...
usageEn: For **although**, take a model sentence like **Although he is very tired, he still went to the library.** (Although he is very tired, he still went to the library.) and replace only the variable information.
For **but**, take a model sentence like **He studies well, but his health is not good.** (He studies well, but his health is not good.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: mainly in explanations and arguments.
examplesEn: Although he is very tired, he still went to the library.
He studies well, but his health is not good.
Although he is intelligent, he is not always correct.
We should go home early, but it is very nice outside.
notesEn: Although — Concession: Although...
but — Contrast: But...
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk2_140.objectiveEn: The **cause-result complex phrases** (Cause-result complex phrases) connect a cause and a result: Because... so... / Due to... Therefore... / Since... Then...
usageEn: For **because**, take a model sentence like **Because it is raining, we canceled the picnic.** (Because it is raining, we canceled the picnic.) and replace only the variable information.
For **so**, take a model sentence like **Because he is sick, he did not come to class.** (Because he is sick, he did not come to class.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: mainly in explanations and arguments.
examplesEn: Because it is raining, we canceled the picnic.
Because he is sick, he did not come to class.
He is sick, so he did not come to class.
She arrived late, so she missed the morning meeting.
notesEn: Because — Cause: Because...
so — Result: So...
**because** — because. The word "because" is used to indicate the reason or explanation of an event or situation. Common combinations observed: 了 (aspect/changing), 没/没有 (negation of the past).

**so** — Result: So... The word "so" is used to indicate a logical conclusion or natural result based on previous facts. Common combinations observed: 了 (aspect/changing), 过 (experience), 没/没有 (negation of the past).

hsk2_141.objectiveEn: Here we deal with **complex sentences** (复句): how to link two propositions (cause, condition, contrast, purpose, etc).
usageEn: Start with a model phrase like **就是** (is) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model phrase (**就是**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
Context: especially in explanations and arguments.
notesEn: 就 — Consequence/ immediacy: Very versatile; often with conditional or rapidity structures (一…就…).
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete phrase to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_159.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **别的 / 其他** and their placement in the sentence.

For **其他**, take a model sentence like **还有其他的吗？** (Are there other things?) and replace only the variable information.

hsk3_160.titleEn: Classifiers Nominatives - (1) Special Noun Classifier: ba, shuang, zhang, zhong...
hsk3_160.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **把 / 双 / 张 / 种** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Start with the patron: **把**.
Context: useful when you want to highlight the treatment and outcome related to the object.
examplesEn: I finished my homework (putting the object 'homework' in focus).
We need to open our minds to solve this problem.
He let his hair grow long.
I cut apples with a knife.
notesEn: 把 — Disposal: Places the object before the verb; very natural if the action has a clear result/impact on the object.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_161.titleEn: Classifiers Nominatives - (2) Borrowed Noun Classifier: wǎn, pán

Context: mainly in questions and responses.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_162.titleEn: Classifiers of Action - Kou, hui, bian, sheng
hsk3_162.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **口 / 回 / 遍 / 声** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Placement: the standard structure is number/demonstrative + classifier + noun.
For **口**, take a model sentence like **他咬了一口苹果。** (He bit a piece of apple.) and replace only the variable information.
For **回**, take a model sentence like **我回家了。** (I went home.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: He bit a piece of apple.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_163.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **个个 / 张张** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Placement: the standard structure is number/demonstrative + classifier + noun.
Context: use this structure in complete sentences related to a concrete situation.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_164.titleEn: Adverbs of Degree - Compare, hái3, gèn, tèbié...
hsk3_164.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **比较 / 还 / 更 / 特别** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Placement: adverbs generally go before the verb or adjective they modify (often after the subject).
For **比较**, take a model sentence like **比较这两个物品。** (Compare these two objects.) and replace only the variable information.
Context: mainly in the nuance of degree, time, or frequency.
examplesEn: A certain number of books are red.
The more you practice, the more you progress.
We can compare these two colors.
notesEn: 比 — Comparison: A + 比 + B + adj/VP : more... than...
更 — Even more: Reinforces an implicit/explicit comparison.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_165.titleEn: Adverbs of Scope - 就3、一块儿、一共、只…
hsk3_165.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **就 / 一块儿 / 一共 / 只** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Placement: Adverbs generally precede the verb or adjective they modify (often after the subject).
Copy a model sentence (**一共多少钱？**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
Context: Especially in questions and responses, and to emphasize degree, time, or frequency.
examplesEn: It's just that I was passing by.
notesEn: 就 — Consequence / immediacy: Very versatile; often with conditional or rapidity structures (一…就…).
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk3_166.titleEn: Adverbs of Time - 才1、马上、先、一会儿…
hsk3_166.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **才 / 马上 / 先 / 一会儿** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Placement: Adverbs generally precede the verb or adjective they modify (often after the subject).
For **才**, take a model sentence like **下午两点他才起床** (He only woke up at 2 PM) and replace only the variable information.
Context: Especially in degree, time, or frequency nuances.
examplesEn: He only woke up at 2 PM
I'll be back in a moment.
notesEn: 才 — Only then: Marks the necessary condition or
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk3_171.titleEn: Adverbs of Negation - 不必、不用
hsk3_171.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **不必 / 不用** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: Placement: Adverbs generally precede the verb or adjective they modify (often after the subject).
For **不必**, take a model sentence like **不必担心** (Inutile de s'inquiéter) and replace only the variable information.
For **不用**, take a model sentence like **不用谢** (Pas de problème) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and timing of the sentence.
Context: especially in shades of degree, time, or frequency.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_172.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **为 / 向 / 关于** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **为**, take a model sentence like **他为了家人努力工作。** (He works hard for his family.) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**他为了家人努力工作。**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and timing of the sentence.
Context: especially in questions and answers.
examplesEn: He works hard for his family.
She worried a lot about this matter.
Why didn't you tell me earlier?
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_173.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **向** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **向**, take a model sentence like **向...学习** (Learn from...) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**向...学习**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_174.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **为了 / 为** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **为了**, take a model sentence like **我为了学习法语。** (I came to learn French.) and replace only the variable information.
For **为**, take a model sentence like **我为了学习法语。** (I came to learn French.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and timing of the sentence.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: He studied hard for the exam.
He works hard for his family.
She worried a lot about this matter.
notesEn: 了 — Aspect / change of state: Marks an accomplished event or a change in situation. Note: 了 is not required in all past tense sentences.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_175.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **把 / 被** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **把**, take a model sentence like **我把作业写完了。** (I finished my homework (putting the object 'homework' in focus).) and replace only the variable information.
For **被**, take a model sentence like **我的手机被人偷了。** (My phone was stolen by someone.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and timing of the sentence.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: I finished my homework (putting the object 'homework' in focus).
My phone was stolen by someone.
I cut apples with a knife.
He carries an umbrella in his hand.
notesEn: 把 — Disposal: Put the object before the verb; very natural if the action has a clear result/impact on the object.
被 — Passive: Focuses on what is suffered; the agent can be omitted if clear.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_176.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **根据** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **根据**, take a model sentence like **根据你说的** (According to what you say) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**根据你说的**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
Context: especially in formal register and information.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_183.titleEn: Four-character expressions - Not A Not B
hsk3_183.objectiveEn: Four-character expressions (四字格) are frequent in written language and in a formal style. They condense an idea (often idiomatic).
usageEn: Use **Not A Not B** as a fixed expression, without changing the character order.
Placement: place **Not A Not B** at the beginning of a sentence or in an aside, just before the idea you are commenting on.
Context: use this formula to introduce an opinion, a reaction, or a transition in a conversation.
Start by memorizing the overall meaning, then reuse the formula in complete sentences.
Insert the formula in a complete sentence without breaking it or rearranging it.
For 四字格, prioritize contexts written/formal or citations.
commonMistakesEn: Do not break the expression into isolated words; it functions as a block.
Avoid literal translation and learn the overall meaning in context first.
Reuse the formula in a complete sentence, not as a single word.

usageEn: Context: use this structure in complete sentences related to a concrete situation.

usageEn: For **seems**, take a model sentence like **He won't come** (Apparemly, he won't come) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**He won't come**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.

hsk3_187.objectiveEn: Here we deal with types of syntagms (nominal, verbal, adjectival phrases) and their structure.
usageEn: Copy a model sentence (**You practice more, you progress more**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.

hsk3_188.objectiveEn: Here we deal with types of syntagms (nominal, verbal, adjectival phrases) and their structure.

hsk3_192.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **… also / … all** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **also**, take a model sentence like **except him, nobody came.** (Except him, nobody came.) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**except him, nobody came.**) and replace the subject/objects/time keeping the same structure.

hsk3_193.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **from … to** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **from**, take a model sentence like **from home to school** (From home to school) and replace only the variable information.
For **to**, take a model sentence like **get up** (wake up) and replace only the variable information.

hsk3_194.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **for …** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **for**, take a model sentence like **for him I speak correctly.** (For him, I speak correctly.) and replace only the variable information.

hsk3_195.objectiveEn: Understand the usage of **一……也 / 都 / 不 / 没……** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **都+不**.

examplesEn: You can do it like this as well.

hsk3_196.objectiveEn: Understand the usage of **一点儿也不** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: Context: use this structure in complete sentences related to a specific situation.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.

hsk3_197.objectiveEn: Understand the usage of **越……越……** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **越**, take a model sentence like **你越练习，越进步。** (The more you practice, the more you progress.) and replace only the variable information.

hsk3_198.objectiveEn: Understand the usage of **（在）……以前 / 以后 / 前 / 后** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **在**, take a model sentence like **门在房子的后面。** (The door is behind the house.) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**门在房子的后面。**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.

hsk3_199.objectiveEn: Understand the usage of **X什么（啊）** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **什么**, take a model sentence like **他有什么东西吗？** (Does he have something?) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**他有什么东西吗？**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: especially in questions and answers.

hsk3_200.objectiveEn: Understand the usage of **该……了** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **该**, take a model sentence like **该怎么办？** (What should we do?) and replace only the variable information.
For **了**, take a model sentence like **吃了饭再去图书馆。** (Eat first, then go to the library.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: especially in questions and answers.
examplesEn: Eat first, then go to the library.
notesEn: 了 — Aspect / change of state: Indicates an accomplished event or a change in the situation. Note: 了 is not required in all past sentences.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_201.objectiveEn: Comprendre l'emploi de **在……上 / 下 / 中** et leur place dans la phrase.
usageEn: Pour **在**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **我在这里。** (Je suis ici.) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Pour **上**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **我们上楼。** (Nous montons l'escalier.) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Contexte : employer cette structure pour décrire des faits ou des actions dans une phrase complète.
examplesEn: Un chat est sous la table.
Nous avons une réunion la semaine prochaine.
notesEn: 在 — Progressif : Avec V : 正在/在 + V indique une action en cours. Avec lieu : 在 + lieu.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk3_204.titleEn: Épithète / expansion du nom - 多项定语
hsk3_204.objectiveEn: Ici on explique un **rôle dans la phrase** (sujet, complément, attribut, etc.) : position, contraintes, et construction.
usageEn: Contexte : utiliser cette structure dans des phrases complètes liées à une situation concrète.

hsk3_205.titleEn: Complément de résultat - 动词+到、住、走、上
hsk3_205.objectiveEn: Les **compléments de résultat** (结果补语) indiquent l'issue d'une action (做完, 写好, 听懂, 关上…).
usageEn: Partir du patron : **动词+到**.
Placement : garder ce qui précède 动词 avant le verbe, et ce qui suit 动词 après le verbe.
Contexte : surtout en questions et réponses.
examplesEn: Combien de temps faut-il pour marcher d'ici ?
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk3_206.titleEn: Complément directionnel - 趋向补语的引申用法：（1）表示结果意义： 动词+出、起、下
hsk3_206.objectiveEn: Les **compléments directionnels** (趋向补语) expriment la direction du mouvement (来/去, 上来/下去/进去/出来…) et, par extension, certains sens figurés (想起来, 发展下去).
hsk3_206.structureEn: 趋向补语的引申用法：（1）表示结果意义： 动词+出
usageEn: Partir du patron : **（1）表示结果意义: 动词+出**.
Placement : garder ce qui précède 动词 avant le verbe, et ce qui suit 动词 après le verbe.
Contexte : employer cette structure pour décrire des faits ou des actions dans une phrase complète.
examplesEn: Un chat est sous la table.
Nous avons une réunion la semaine prochaine.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk3_207.titleEn: Directional Complement - (2) Expressing the Start of an Action: Verb + Up
hsk3_207.objectiveEn: Directional complements (趋向补语) express the direction of movement (来/去, 上来/下去/进去/出来…) and, by extension, certain figurative meanings (想起来, 发展下去).
hsk3_207.structureEn: (2) Expressing the Start of an Action: Verb + Up
usageEn: Starting Point: **Verb + Up**.
Placement: keep what precedes the verb before the verb, and what follows the verb after the verb.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions within a complete sentence.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_208.titleEn: Directional Complement - (3) Expressing the Continuation of an Action: Verb + Down
hsk3_208.objectiveEn: Directional complements (趋向补语) express the direction of movement (来/去, 上来/下去/进去/出来…) and, by extension, certain figurative meanings (想起来, 发展下去).
hsk3_208.structureEn: (3) Expressing the Continuation of an Action: Verb + Down
usageEn: Starting Point: **Verb + Down**.
Placement: keep what precedes the verb before the verb, and what follows the verb after the verb.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions within a complete sentence.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_209.titleEn: Possibility Complement - Verb + 得、不 + Verb, Adjective
hsk3_209.objectiveEn: Possibility complements (可能补语) express if an action is possible: V + 得/不 + complement (看得懂/看不懂).
usageEn: Starting Point: **Verb + 得**.
Placement: follow the strict order of blocks in the structure, from left to right.
For **得**, take a model sentence like **这个字我看得懂。** (I can understand this character.) and replace only the variable information.
For **不**, take a model sentence like **不会唱歌** (cannot sing) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions within a complete sentence.
examplesEn: You should go to bed early
Tomorrow I have to go to work.
notesEn: 得 — Complement: Introduces a complement after the verb (说得很好).

**得** — particle of complement (result; manner). The word "devoir" in French can be used to express obligations, whether they are related to law, rules, or ethics. Common combinations observed: 得 (degree/complement).
**不** — not... not. The character "不" is used to form negations in Chinese. Common combinations observed: 不 (general negation).

hsk3_210.objectiveEn: Here we explain a role in the sentence (subject, complement, attribute, etc.): position, constraints, and construction.
usageEn: Starting Point: **(1) Adjective + 得很**.
Placement: strictly follow the order of blocks in the structure, from left to right.
Context: use this structure in complete sentences related to a concrete situation.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_211.titleEn: Degree Complement - (2) Adjective, Verb + 极了、坏了
hsk3_211.objectiveEn: Here we explain a role in the sentence (subject, complement, attribute, etc.): position, constraints, and construction.
usageEn: Starting Point: **(2) Adjective**.
Context: use this structure in complete sentences related to a concrete situation.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_212.titleEn: Quantitative Complement - Expressing the Interval Time from the End of an Action to a Certain Time Point
hsk3_212.objectiveEn: Quantitative complements (数量补语) indicate how much / how many times / how long: V + 数量 + (名/次/遍/分钟…), or V + 了 + 时量…
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk3_213.objectiveEn: Interrogative phrases cover questions in 吗, questions A不A, and interrogative words (什么, 哪儿, 怎么, 为什么…).
usageEn: Start with the pattern: 'how + verb' specific questions.
Placement: keep the verb before the verb for what precedes 动词, and after the verb for what follows 动词.
Context: mainly in questions and answers, questions, reactions, and modalities.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk3_214.titleEn: Question rhétorique - 用反问句表示强调：不是……吗？
hsk3_214.objectiveEn: Les **questions rhétoriques** (反问句) servent à affirmer avec force. On y trouve souvent 难道 / 不是…吗 / 怎么…呢.
usageEn: Pour **吗**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **你吃饭了吗？** (As-tu mangé ?) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Copier une phrase modèle (**你吃饭了吗？**) puis remplacer sujet/objet/temps en gardant la même charpente.
Après substitution, vérifier que 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 restent cohérents avec le sens et le temps de la phrase.
Contexte : surtout en questions et réponses et questions, réactions et modalité.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk3_215.titleEn: Structure 把 - (1) Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + In/To + Place
hsk3_215.objectiveEn: The **structure in 把** (disposal) serves to put the **object** and the **result/impact** of the action in focus. It is natural when the object is **defined** (known from the context) and the verb is followed by a **complement** (result, direction, duration, quantity, etc.).
hsk3_215.structureEn: (1) Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + In/To + Place
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **(1) Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + In/To**.
Placement: in the 把 structure, place 把 + complement before the main verb, then keep the result after the verb.
For **把**, take a model sentence like **我把作业写完了。** (I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).) and replace only the variable information.
For **In/To**, take a model sentence like **我在这里。** (I am here.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, check that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: useful when wanting to highlight the treatment and outcome related to the object.
examplesEn: I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).
I cut apples with a knife.
He wears an umbrella in his hand.
notesEn: In — Progressive: With V: 正在/在 + V indicates an ongoing action. With place: 在 + place.
把 — Disposal: Places the object before the main verb; very natural when the action has a clear result/impact on the object.
**把** : (particle marking the object). The word "把" is used to indicate the action of taking or holding something, often when this thing has a handle or a handle.
**在** : being at; in progress. This verb means "to exist" or "to be alive".
**到** : until. The character 到 translates as "to" or "to reach". Common combinations observed: 了 (aspect/changing).

hsk3_216.titleEn: Structure 把 - （2）主语+把+宾语1+动词（+给）+宾语2
hsk3_216.objectiveEn: The **structure in 把** (disposal) serves to put the **object** and the **result/impact** of the action in focus. It is natural when the object is **defined** (known from the context) and the verb is followed by a **complement** (result, direction, duration, quantity, etc.).
hsk3_216.structureEn: (2) Subject + 把 + Object 1 + Verb (+ to) + Object 2
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **(2) Subject + 把 + Object 1 + Verb (+ to) + Object 2**.
Placement: in the 把 structure, place 把 + complement before the main verb, then keep the result after the verb.
For **把**, take a model phrase like **我把作业写完了。** (I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, check that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: useful when wanting to highlight the treatment and outcome related to the object.
examplesEn: I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).
I cut apples with a knife.
He carries an umbrella in his hand.
notesEn: 把 — Disposal: Places the object before the verb; very natural when the action has a clear result/impact on the object.

**把** : (particle marking the object). The word "把" is used to indicate the action of taking or holding something, often when this thing has a handle or a handle. Frequent combinations observed: 了 (aspect/change), 着 (durative), 把 (highlighting the object).

hsk3_217.titleEn: Structure 把 - （3）主语+把+宾语+动词+结果补语、趋向补语、状态补语
hsk3_217.objectiveEn: The **structure in 把** (disposal) serves to put the **object** and the **result/resultation** of the action in focus. It is natural when the object is **defined** (known from the context) and the verb is followed by a **complement** (result, direction, duration, quantity, etc.).
hsk3_217.structureEn: (3) Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + Result Complement
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **(3) Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + Result Complement**.
Placement: in the 把 structure, place 把 + complement before the main verb, then keep the result after the verb.
For **把**, take a model phrase like **我把作业写完了。** (I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, check that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: useful when wanting to highlight the treatment and outcome related to the object.
examplesEn: I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).
I cut apples with a knife.
He carries an umbrella in his hand.
notesEn: 把 — Disposal: Places the object before the verb; very natural if the action has a clear result/impact on the object.

**把** : (particle marking the object). The word "把" is used to indicate the action of taking or holding something, often when this thing has a handle or a handle. Frequent combinations observed: 了 (aspect/change), 着 (durative), 把 (highlighting the object).

hsk3_218.titleEn: Voix passive - （1）主语+被+宾语+动词+其他成分
hsk3_218.objectiveEn: Les **formes passives** (被 / 让 / 叫) mettent l'accent sur ce qui est **subi** par le sujet. On les emploie surtout quand l'événement est **marquant** (souvent négatif, mais pas toujours) ou quand on veut mettre en avant la conséquence pour le sujet.
usageEn: Partir du patron : **（1）主语+被+宾语+动词+其他成分**.
Placement : dans la passive en 被, place 被 avant le verbe, l'agent éventuel se met entre 被 et le verbe.
Pour **被**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **我的手机被人偷了。** (On m'a volé mon téléphone.) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Après substitution, vérifier que 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 restent cohérents avec le sens et le temps de la phrase.
Contexte : utiliser la forme passive quand on focalise sur ce qui est subi.
notesEn: 被 — Passif : Met l'accent sur ce qui est subi; l'agent peut être omis si évident.
**被** : par (passif). Le caractère 被 est utilisé pour exprimer une action passive. Combinaisons fréquentes observées : 了 (aspect/changement), 被 (passif).

hsk3_224.objectiveEn: Les **phrases comparatives** permettent d'exprimer une comparaison (supériorité, égalité, différence, progression…). Les marqueurs typiques sont **比**, **跟…一样**, **没有**, **越来越**, **越…越…**.
usageEn: Partir du patron : **（4）A不比B+形容词**.
Placement : dans une comparaison en 比, place A + 比 + B avant l'adjectif ou le verbe comparé.
Remplacer **A**, **B** par tes mots/nombres, sans changer l'ordre.
Ne pas permuter **不比** et **不比形容词** : choisir l'élément selon le sens visé.
Contexte : utiliser pour comparer explicitement deux éléments (niveau, qualité ou quantité).
examplesEn: Aujourd'hui il fait plus froid qu'hier.
notesEn: 比 — Comparaison : A + 比 + B + adj/VP : plus… que…

hsk3_225.titleEn: Phrases comparatives - （5）A比B+多、少、早、晚+动词+数量短语
hsk3_225.objectiveEn: Les **phrases comparatives** permettent d'exprimer une comparaison (supériorité, égalité, différence, progression…). Les marqueurs typiques sont **比**, **跟…一样**, **没有**, **越来越**, **越…越…**.
usageEn: Partir du patron : **（5）A比B+多**.
Placement : suivre strictement l'ordre des blocs du patron, de gauche à droite.
Remplacer **A**, **B** par tes mots/nombres, sans changer l'ordre.
Pour **比**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **今天比昨天冷。** (Aujourd'hui il fait plus froid qu'hier.) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Pour **多**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **多数人喜欢这个电影。** (Beaucoup de personnes aiment ce film.) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Ne pas permuter **比** et **多** : choisir l'élément selon le sens visé.
Contexte : utiliser pour comparer explicitement deux éléments (niveau, qualité ou quantité).
examplesEn: Aujourd'hui il fait plus froid qu'hier.
Beaucoup de personnes aiment ce film.
Il manque un livre chez moi.
Une minorité d'entre eux est d'accord avec cette opinion.
notesEn: 比 — Comparaison : A + 比 + B + adj/VP : plus… que…
**比** : comparer.
**多** : beaucoup. Le mot "多" signifie "beaucoup", "plus" ou "une grande quantité".
**少** : peu. Le mot "少" signifie littéralement "peu".
**早** : tôt. Le mot "早" peut signifier "matin", "tôt".
**晚** : tard. Le caractère "晚" signifie "soir".

hsk3_243.titleEn: Expressions spéciales (数的表达法) - 概数表达法2：（1）用“大概”表示概数
hsk3_243.objectiveEn: Expressions spéciales (数的表达法): formes essentielles, usage et position dans la phrase.
hsk3_243.structureEn: 概数表达法2：（1）用“大概”表示概数
usageEn: Partir du patron : **（1）用“大概”表示概数**.
Contexte : surtout en chiffres, quantités et données.
examplesEn: On peut probablement ignorer ce problème.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk3_244.titleEn: Expressions spéciales (数的表达法) - （2）相邻数词连用表示概数
hsk3_244.objectiveEn: Expressions spéciales (数的表达法): formes essentielles, usage et position dans la phrase.
usageEn: Partir du patron : **（2）相邻数词连用表示概数**.
Contexte : surtout en chiffres, quantités et données.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk4_245.objectiveEn: Comprendre l'emploi de **敢** et leur place dans la phrase.
usageEn: Placement : le verbe principal se place en général après le sujet et avant l'objet.
Pour **敢**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **你敢吗？** (Tu oses ?) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Après substitution, vérifier que 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 restent cohérents avec le sens et le temps de la phrase.
Contexte : surtout en questions et réponses.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk4_246.objectiveEn: Comprendre l'emploi de **各 / 各位 / 各种 / 任何** et leur place dans la phrase.
usageEn: Pour **各**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **各种** (différents) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Pour **各位**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **各位好** (Bonjour à tous) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Contexte : employer cette structure pour décrire des faits ou des actions dans une phrase complète.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk4_247.titleEn: Classificateurs nominaux - （1）专用名量词：打、袋、棵、台…
hsk4_247.objectiveEn: Comprendre l'emploi de **打 / 袋 / 棵 / 台** et leur place dans la phrase.
usageEn: Partir du patron : **打**.
Contexte : employer cette structure pour décrire des faits ou des actions dans une phrase complète.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk4_248.titleEn: Classificateurs nominaux - （2）借用名量词：脸、手、盒、屋子…
hsk4_248.objectiveEn: Comprendre l'emploi de **脸 / 手 / 盒 / 屋子** et leur place dans la phrase.
usageEn: Partir du patron : **脸**.
Contexte : employer cette structure pour décrire des faits ou des actions dans une phrase complète.
examplesEn: Ils s'assoient à côté de la table pour discuter.
Il y a un livre sur la table.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk4_254.titleEn: Adverbes de manière - 故意、互相、相互
hsk4_254.objectiveEn: Comprendre l'emploi de **故意 / 互相 / 相互** et leur place dans la phrase.
usageEn: Placement : les adverbes se placent en général avant le verbe ou l'adjectif qu'ils modifient (souvent après le sujet).
Pour **故意**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **不是故意的** (Ce n'est pas fait exprès) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Pour **互相**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **互相帮助** (s'aider mutuellement) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Après substitution, vérifier que 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 restent cohérents avec le sens et le temps de la phrase.
Contexte : surtout en nuance de degré, de temps ou de fréquence.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk4_255.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **却** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Placement: Adverbs generally precede the verb or adjective they modify (often after the subject).
For **却**, take a model sentence like **他虽然努力了，却没能完成任务。** (Although he worked hard, he failed to complete the task.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: Especially in explanation and argumentation and nuance of degree, time, or frequency.
examplesEn: Although he worked hard, he failed to complete the task.
However, it turns out he is a good person.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_256.titleEn: Adverbs of Manner - 也许、恐怕
hsk4_256.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **也许 / 恐怕** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Placement: Adverbs generally precede the verb or adjective they modify (often after the subject).
For **也许**, take a model sentence like **也许是** (It might be) and replace only the variable information.
For **恐怕**, take a model sentence like **恐怕是** (It might be) and replace only the variable information.
Context: Especially in oral interaction and instructions and nuance of degree, time, or frequency.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_257.titleEn: Adverbs of Attitude - 才4、就5、并1、还4…
hsk4_257.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **才 / 就 / 并 / 还** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Placement: Adverbs generally precede the verb or adjective they modify (often after the subject).
For **才**, take a model sentence like **下午两点他才起床** (He only woke up at two o'clock) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: Especially in questions and answers and explanation and argumentation.
examplesEn: He only woke up at two o'clock
He likes reading and watching movies.
Despite the rain, they went to the park, which is still.
He won the competition, which is surprising.
notesEn: 就 — Consequence / immediacy: Very versatile; often with conditional or rapid structures (一…就…).
才 — Only then: Marks the necessary condition or 'only at that moment'.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_258.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **自 / 当 / 由 / 随着** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **自**, take a model sentence like **自从你来之后，情况就不同了。** (Since you came, the situation has changed.) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**自从你来之后，情况就不同了。**) and replace subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: Especially in explanation and argumentation.
examplesEn: Since you came, the situation has changed.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_259.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **将 / 由 / 叫 / 让** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Copy a model sentence (**她在叫救命。**) and replace subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
Context: Use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: Let the children play a bit.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_260.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **由于** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **由于**, take a model sentence like **由于...所以...** (Because... so...) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**由于...所以...**) and replace subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
Context: Especially in explanation and argumentation.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_261.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **for / with** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **for**, take a model sentence like **for this question, I have some opinions.** (Concerning this question, I have some opinions.) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**for this question, I have some opinions.**) and replace the subject/objec/tense while keeping the same structure.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: Concerning this question, I have some opinions.
About these weather changes, we need to be prepared.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_262.titleEn: Introduce Means/Base - As, According to, By
hsk4_262.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **as / according to / by** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **as**, take a model sentence like **as a friend** (As an friend) and replace only the variable information.
For **according to**, take a model sentence like **according to the doorbell** (According to the doorbell) and replace only the variable information.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_263.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **and / but / and** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **and**, take a model sentence like **he likes reading and watching movies.** (He likes reading and watching movies.) and replace only the variable information.
For **but**, take a model sentence like **he woke up and went to the bathroom.** (He woke up and went to the bathroom.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / not / no /吗 remain coherent with the sense and time of the sentence.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: He likes reading and watching movies.
He woke up and went to the bathroom.
It was late, but he continued to play.
These fruits and vegetables are all very healthy.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_264.titleEn: Connect Propositions/Phrases - Moreover, But 2, Since, Even...
hsk4_264.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **Moreover / But / Since / Even** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **Moreover**, take a model sentence like **Moreover, he can also speak English.** (Moreover, he can also speak English.) and replace only the variable information.
For **But**, take a model sentence like **He woke up and went to the bathroom.** (He woke up and went to the bathroom.) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / not / no /吗 remain coherent with the sense and time of the sentence.
Context: especially in explanation and argumentation, and in oral interaction and instructions.
examplesEn: Moreover, he can also speak English.
He woke up and went to the bathroom.
Since you came, let's go to the party together.
We have already done our best, even if we can try again.
It was late, but he continued to play.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_265.titleEn: Connect Propositions/Phrases - Not Only, Not Only, Besides, If...
hsk4_265.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **Not only / Not only / Besides / If** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **Not only**, take a model sentence like **not only...but also...** (Not only...but also...) and replace only the variable information.
For **Not only**, take a model sentence like **not only...but also...** (Not only...but also...) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / not / no /吗 remain coherent with the sense and time of the sentence.
Context: especially in explanation and argumentation.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_266.titleEn: Other Particles - Superior, Also, Besides, If
hsk4_266.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **superior / also / besides / if** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Placement: the particle is placed immediately after the word or group it marks.
For **superior**, take a model sentence like **we go upstairs.** (We go upstairs.) and replace only the variable information.
For **also**, take a model sentence like **Beijing, Shanghai, etc.** (Beijing, Shanghai, and other cities) and replace only the variable information.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_267.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **啊 / 嗯** and their placement in a sentence.
usageEn: For **啊**, take a model sentence like **真啊，你还会说中文。** (Ah ! You still know how to speak Chinese.) and replace only the variable information.
For **嗯**, take a model sentence like **嗯，我知道了** (Yes, I understand) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: especially in questions and answers.
examplesEn: Ah ! You still know how to speak Chinese.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of the elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_268.titleEn: Types of structures - Numerals + Adjectives + Measure Words
hsk4_268.objectiveEn: Here we are dealing with **types of syntagms** (nominal, verbal, adjectival phrases, etc.) and their structure.
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **Numerals + Adjectives + Measure Words**.
Placement: keep the fixed order **Numerals → Adjectives → Measure Words**; place **Numerals** at the beginning of the segment and **Measure Words** at the end.
Context: use this structure in complete sentences related to a concrete situation.
notesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.
commonMistakesEn: Verify the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_269.objectiveEn: Four-character expressions (Four-character group) are frequent in written language and in a formal style. They condense a concept (often idiomatic).
notesEn: Do not break the expression into isolated words: it functions as a block.
Avoid literal translation and learn the overall meaning in context first.
Reuse the formula in a complete sentence, not as a single word.
commonMistakesEn: Do not break the expression into isolated words: it functions as a block.
Avoid literal translation and learn the overall meaning in context first.
Reuse the formula in a complete sentence, not as a single word.

hsk4_270.titleEn: Formules en quatre caractères - 无A无B
hsk4_270.objectiveEn: Les **expressions en quatre caractères** (四字格) sont fréquentes à l'écrit et dans un style soutenu. Elles condensent une idée (souvent quasi-idiomatique).
usageEn: Utiliser **无A无B** comme une expression figée, sans changer l'ordre des caractères.
Placement : placer **无A无B** en tête de phrase ou en incise, juste avant l'idée que tu commentes.
Contexte : utiliser cette formule pour introduire un avis, une réaction ou une transition dans un échange.
Commencer par mémoriser le sens global, puis réemployer la formule dans des phrases complètes.
Insérer la formule en bloc dans une phrase complète, sans la découper ni la réordonner.
Pour les 四字格, privilégier les contextes écrits/soutenus ou les citations.
commonMistakesEn: Ne pas découper l'expression en mots isolés : elle fonctionne comme un bloc.
Éviter la traduction mot à mot et apprendre d'abord le sens global en contexte.
Réutiliser la formule dans une phrase complète, pas comme un mot seul.

hsk4_271.titleEn: Four-character expressions - Have A Have B
hsk4_271.objectiveEn: Four-character expressions (四字格) are frequent in written language and in a formal style. They condense a meaning (often idiomatic).
usageEn: Use **Have A Have B** as a fixed expression, without changing the character order.
Placement: place **Have A Have B** at the beginning of a sentence or in an aside, just before the idea you are commenting on.
Context: use this formula to introduce an opinion, a reaction, or a transition in a dialogue exchange.
Start by memorizing the overall meaning, then reuse the formula in complete sentences.
Insert the formula as a whole into a complete sentence, without breaking it or rearranging it.
For 四字格, prioritize contexts in written or formal language or citations.
notesEn: Do not break the expression into isolated words; it functions as a block.
Avoid literal translation and learn the overall meaning in context first.
Reuse the formula in a complete sentence, not as a single word.
commonMistakesEn: Do not break the expression into individual words; it functions as a whole.
Avoid word-for-word translation and learn the overall meaning in context first.
Reuse the formula as a complete sentence, not as a single word.

hsk4_272.objectiveEn: Ici on traite des **types de syntagmes** (phrases nominales, verbales, adjectivales…) et de leur structure.
usageEn: Utiliser **来得及、来不及** comme une expression figée, sans changer l'ordre des caractères.
Placement : placer **来得及、来不及** en tête de phrase ou en incise, juste avant l'idée que tu commentes.
Contexte : utiliser cette formule pour introduire un avis, une réaction ou une transition dans un échange.
Commencer par mémoriser le sens global, puis réemployer la formule dans des phrases complètes.
Prendre une phrase modèle (**在口语里，常听到“来得及、来不及”这种说法。**) puis remplacer uniquement le contexte (sujet, temps, situation).
examplesEn: À l'oral, on entend souvent la formule « 来得及、来不及 ».
notesEn: 得 — Complément : Introduit un complément après le verbe (说得很好).
commonMistakesEn: Ne pas découper l'expression en mots isolés : elle fonctionne comme un bloc.
Éviter la traduction mot à mot et apprendre d'abord le sens global en contexte.
Réutiliser la formule dans une phrase complète, pas comme un mot seul.

hsk4_273.objectiveEn: Here we deal with **types of syntagms** (nominal, verbal, adjectival phrases, etc.) and their structure.
usageEn: Use **有的是** as a fixed expression, without changing the character order.
Placement: place **有的是** at the beginning of the sentence or in an incision, just before the idea you comment on.
Context: use this formula to introduce an opinion, a reaction, or a transition in a conversation.
Start by memorizing the global meaning, then reuse the formula in complete sentences.
Take a model sentence (in spoken language, often heard: “在口语里，常听到‘有的是’这种说法。”) and replace only the context (subject, time, situation).
examplesEn: In spoken language, we often hear the phrase ‘有的是’.
commonMistakesEn: Do not break the expression into isolated words: it functions as a block.
Avoid literal translation and learn the global meaning in context first.
Reuse the formula in a complete sentence, not as a single word.

hsk4_274.objectiveEn: Here we deal with **types of syntagms** (nominal, verbal, adjectival phrases, etc.) and their structure.
usageEn: Use **说不定** as a fixed expression, without changing the character order.
Placement: place **说不定** at the beginning of the sentence or in an incision, just before the idea you comment on.
Context: use this formula to introduce an opinion, a reaction, or a transition in a conversation.
Start by memorizing the global meaning, then reuse the formula in complete sentences.
Take a model sentence (in spoken language, often heard: “在口语里，常听到‘说不定’这种说法。”) and replace only the context (subject, time, situation).
examplesEn: In spoken language, we often hear the phrase ‘说不定’.
commonMistakesEn: Do not break the expression into isolated words: it functions as a block.
Avoid literal translation and learn the global meaning in context first.
Reuse the formula in a complete sentence, not as a single word.

hsk4_277.titleEn: Fixed Phrases - 一+量词+比+一+量词
hsk4_277.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **一 / 量词 / 比** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: Start from the pattern: **一+量词+比+一+量词**.
Placement: keep the fixed order **一 → 量词 → 比 → 一 → 量词**; place **一** at the beginning of the segment and **量词** at the end.
Context: use for explicit comparison of two elements (level, quality, or quantity).
notesEn: 比 — Comparison: A + 比 + B + adj/VP: more... than...
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_278.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **在……方面** and their placement in the sentence.
usageEn: For **在**, take a model sentence like **我在这里。** (I am here.) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (in spoken language, often heard: “我在这里。”) and replace the subject/objec/times in keeping the same structure.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
notesEn: 在 — Progressive: With V: 正在/在 + V indicates an ongoing action. With place: 在 + place.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_285.objectiveEn: Point de grammaire essentiel à maîtriser pour produire des phrases correctes.
usageEn: Pour **没**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **我没有钱** (Je n'ai pas d'argent) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Copier une phrase modèle (**我没有钱**) puis remplacer sujet/objet/temps en gardant la même charpente.
Après substitution, vérifier que 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 restent cohérents avec le sens et le temps de la phrase.
Contexte : employer cette structure pour décrire des faits ou des actions dans une phrase complète.
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

hsk4_286.objectiveEn: Comprendre l'emploi de **X是X / Y是Y** et leur place dans la phrase.
usageEn: Pour **是**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **他是学生。** (Il est étudiant.) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.

hsk4_288.objectiveEn: Comprendre l'emploi de **X就是了** et leur place dans la phrase.
usageEn: Contexte : utiliser cette structure dans des phrases complètes liées à une situation concrète.

usageEn: Pour **呢**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **你 看 这个 包 呢？** (Tu regardes ce sac, non ?) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Copier une phrase modèle (**你 看 这个 包 呢？**) puis remplacer sujet/objet/temps en gardant la même charpente.

hsk4_290.objectiveEn: Comprendre l'emploi de **你X你的吧** et leur place dans la phrase.
usageEn: Pour **你**, reprends une phrase modèle comme **你是老师。** (Tu es professeur.) puis remplace seulement les informations variables.
Copier une phrase modèle (**你是老师。**) puis remplacer sujet/objet/temps en gardant la même charpente.

hsk4_293.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **就** and their place in the sentence.
usageEn: For **就**, take a model sentence like **差一点儿就赢了** (We almost won) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model sentence (**差一点儿就赢了**) and replace the subject/verb/object/verb time keeping the same structure.
Context: Use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: This dish is a little spicy
notesEn: 就 — Consequence / immediacy: Very versatile; often with conditional or rapidity structures (一…就…).

hsk4_294.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **是** and their place in the sentence.
usageEn: For **是**, take a model sentence like **他是学生。** (He is a student.) and replace only the variable information.

Context: Use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_295.titleEn: Degree Complement - Adjectives, Psychological Verbs + 死了, 好厉害
hsk4_295.objectiveEn: Understand the role in the sentence (subject, complement, attribute, etc.): position, constraints, and construction.
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **Psychological Verb + 死了**.
Placement: keep what precedes 动词 before the verb, and what follows 动词 after the verb.
Context: Use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
notesEn: 了 — Aspect / change of state: Marks an accomplished event or a change in situation. Note: 了 is not mandatory in all past sentences.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_296.titleEn: Possibility Complement - Verb + 得、不 + 了
hsk4_296.objectiveEn: Possibility Complements (可能补语) express if an action is possible: V + 得/不 + complement (看得懂/看不懂).
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **动词+得**.
Placement: keep what precedes 动词 before the verb, and what follows 动词 after the verb.
For **得**, take a model sentence like **这个字我看得懂。** (I can understand this character.) and replace only the variable information.
For **不**, take a model sentence like **不会唱歌** (cannot sing) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, check that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: Use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: Let's eat first, then go to the library.
You should go to bed early
notesEn: 了 — Aspect / change of state: Marks an accomplished event or a change in situation. Note: 了 is not mandatory in all past sentences.
得 — Complement: Introduces a complement after the verb (说得很好).

**得** — Complement: Indicates a result or manner. The word "devoir" in French can be used to express obligations, whether they are related to law, rules, or ethics. Common combinations observed: 得 (complement).
**不** — Negation: Forms negations in Chinese. Common combinations observed: 不 (negation).
**了** — Aspect: Marks an accomplished or completed action. Common combinations observed: 了 (aspect).

hsk4_297.objectiveEn: Rhetorical **questions** (反问句) serve to assert forcefully. They often contain 难道 / 不是…吗 / 怎么…呢.
usageEn: For **难道**, take a model sentence like **难道是真的？** (Is it really true?) and replace only the variable information.
For **吗**, take a model sentence like **你吃饭了吗？** (Have you eaten?) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and tense of the sentence.
Context: mainly in questions and responses, questions, reactions, and modality.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_298.titleEn: Structure 把 - (1) 主语+把+宾语+动词（+一、了）+动词
hsk4_298.objectiveEn: The **structure in 把** (disposal) serves to highlight the **object** and the **result/impact** of the action. It is natural when the object is **defined** (known from the context) and the verb is followed by a **complement** (result, direction, duration, quantity, etc.).
hsk4_298.structureEn: (1) Subject + 把 + Object + Verb (+ 了) + Verb
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **(1) Subject + 把 + Object + Verb (+ 了) + Verb**.
Placement: in the 把 structure, place 把 + complement before the main verb, then keep the result after the verb.
For **把**, take a model sentence like **我把作业写完了。** (I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).) and replace only the variable information.
For **了**, take a model sentence like **我把作业写完了。** (I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).) and replace only the variable information.
Do not swap **把** and **了**: choose the element according to the intended meaning.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and tense of the sentence.
Context: useful when wanting to highlight the treatment and outcome related to the object.
examplesEn: I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).
Let's eat first, then go to the library.
I cut apples with a knife.
He carries an umbrella in his hand.
notesEn: 了 — Aspect / state change: Marks an accomplished event or a change in situation. Note: 了 is not required in all past sentences.
把 — Disposal: Places the object before the main verb; very natural if the action has a clear result/impact on the object.
**把** — (particle marking the object). The word "把" is used to indicate taking or holding something, often when this thing has a handle or handle.
**了** — Aspect completed. This particle serves to mark an action that has been completed or finished. Common combinations observed: 了 (aspect/state), 把 (highlighting the object).
commonMistakesEn: Vérifier l'ordre des éléments avant de parler ou d'écrire.
Utiliser une phrase complète pour valider la structure en contexte réel.

For **了**, take a model sentence like **我把作业写完了。** (I finished my homework (making the object 'homework' the focus).) and replace only the variable information.

Context: useful when wanting to highlight the treatment and outcome related to the object.

把 — Disposal: Places the object before the main verb; very natural if the action has a clear result/impact on the object.

**把** — (particle marking the object). The word "把" is used to indicate taking or holding something, often when this thing has a handle or handle. Common combinations observed: 了 (aspect/state), 着 (durative), 把 (highlighting the object).
**了** — Aspect completed. This particle serves to mark an action that has been completed or finished. Common combinations observed: 了 (aspect/state), 把 (highlighting the object).

hsk4_303.titleEn: Passive Voice - Subject + Call, Let + Object + Verb + Other Components
hsk4_303.objectiveEn: The **passive forms** (be / let / call) emphasize what the subject **experiences**. They are mostly used when the event is **noteworthy** (often negative, but not always) or when you want to highlight the consequence for the subject.
hsk4_303.structureEn: Subject + Call Let + Object + Verb + Other Components
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **Subject + Call**.
Placement: strictly follow the order of blocks in the pattern, from left to right.
For **Call**, take a model phrase like **She calls for help.** (She cries for help.) and replace only the variable information.
For **Let**, take a model phrase like **Let the children play a bit.** (Let the children play a bit.) and replace only the variable information.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: Let the children play a bit.
notesEn: Be — Passive: Emphasizes what is experienced; the agent can be omitted if evident.
Call — To call oneself. The Chinese word "call" can mean "call", "cry", or "call oneself".
Let — To let; to do (causative). The word "let" is used to express the action of giving something to someone or allowing someone to do something.

hsk4_304.titleEn: Pivot Construction - (1) Expressing Love and Hatred: Subject + Praise, Criticism + Object 1 + Verb + Object 2
hsk4_304.objectiveEn: The **pivot sentences** (pivot sentences) have an element that is both the object of the 1st verb and the subject of the 2nd (e.g., let him go / ask you to help).
hsk4_304.structureEn: (1) Expressing Love and Hatred: Subject + Praise Criticism + Object 1 + Verb + Object 2
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **Subject + Praise**.
Placement: strictly follow the order of blocks in the pattern, from left to right.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: His drawing is really pretty, I will praise it.
He did very well, you should praise him.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_305.titleEn: Pivot Construction - (2) Expressing Titles or Definitions: Subject + Receive, Select + Object 1 + Do, Be, For + Object 2
hsk4_305.objectiveEn: The **pivot sentences** (pivot sentences) have an element that is both the object of the 1st verb and the subject of the 2nd (e.g., let him go / ask you to help).
hsk4_305.structureEn: (2) Expressing Titles or Definitions: Subject + Receive Select + Object 1 + Do For + Object 2
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **Subject + Receive**.
Placement: strictly follow the order of blocks in the pattern, from left to right.
Context: mostly in questions and answers.
examplesEn: He works hard for his family.
She worried a lot about this matter.
Why didn't you tell me earlier?
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_306.titleEn: Pivot Construction - (3) Expressing Result: Subject + Cause, Let + Person Pronoun + Verb Phrase
hsk4_306.objectiveEn: The **pivot sentences** (pivot sentences) have an element that is both the object of the 1st verb and the subject of the 2nd (e.g., let him go / ask you to help).
hsk4_306.structureEn: (3) Expressing Result: Subject + Cause Let + Person Pronoun + Verb Phrase
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **Subject + Cause**.
Placement: strictly follow the order of blocks in the pattern, from left to right.
Context: mainly in questions and answers.
examplesEn: Let the children play a bit.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in real context.

hsk4_307.titleEn: Comparative Phrases - (1) A is not as good as B (with adjective)
hsk4_307.objectiveEn: The **comparative phrases** (comparative phrases) allow expressing a comparison (superiority, equality, difference, progression...). Typical markers are **compare with**, **not**, **no longer**, **getting... more...**.
hsk4_307.structureEn: (1) A is not as good as B (with adjective)
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **(1) A is not as good as B (with adjective)**.
Placement: strictly follow the order of blocks in the pattern, from left to right.
Replace **A**, **B** with your words/nouns, without changing the order.
For **not**, take a model phrase like **rather than... than...** (Rather than... better than...), and replace only the variable information.
Do not swap **not** and **not adjective**: choose the element according to the intended meaning.
After substitution, verify that 了 / not / no /吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: Rather go to see a movie
He is not as smart as you.
notesEn: Compare — Comparison: A + compare with + B + adj/VP: more... than...
not — not well; rather. Common combinations observed: not (general negation).

hsk4_308.objectiveEn: The **comparative phrases** (comparative phrases) allow expressing a comparison (superiority, equality, difference, progression...). Typical markers are **compare with**, **not**, **no longer**, **getting... more...**.
usageEn: Start with the pattern: **(2) Compared to...**.
For **compared to**, take a model phrase like **compared to...** (Compared to...), and replace only the variable information.
For **compared with**, take a model phrase like **compared with...** (Compared with...), and replace only the variable information.
Context: use for explicit comparison of two elements (level, quality or quantity).
notesEn: Compare — Comparison: A + compare with + B + adj/VP: more... than...
compared to — with; follow.
compared with — compared to.

hsk4_315.objectiveEn: The **complex chaining phrases** (Succession Conjunctions) express succession: 先…然后… / …之后… / 于是… / 随即…
usageEn: For **于是**, take a model phrase like **昨天他生病了，于是他没来上课。** (He was sick yesterday, so he didn't come to class.) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model phrase (**昨天他生病了，于是他没来上课。**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: He was sick yesterday, so he didn't come to class.
He finished his homework, so his mother praised him.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

hsk4_316.objectiveEn: Understand the use of **…… / 甚至……** and their place in the sentence.
usageEn: For **甚至**, take a model phrase like **我们已经尽力了，甚至可以再试一次。** (We have already tried our best, even if we can try again.) and replace only the variable information.
Copy a model phrase (**我们已经尽力了，甚至可以再试一次。**) and replace the subject/object/time while keeping the same structure.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
examplesEn: We have already tried our best, even if we can try again.
Even her grades exceed the maximum score.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.

For **不光**, take a model phrase like **不光...还...** (Non seulement...mais aussi...) and replace only the variable information.
After substitution, verify that 了 / 不 / 没 / 吗 remain coherent with the meaning and time of the sentence.
Context: use this structure to describe facts or actions in a complete sentence.
commonMistakesEn: Check the order of elements before speaking or writing.
Use a complete sentence to validate the structure in a real context.
